Susanna was the daugther of James Buck from Kent England.
Susannah came in the Hercules 1657 in the family of Henry
of Ipswich, Massachusetts. She was with that family

zh.lifeSummary: 當Susanna Buck於1622年7月1日出生在英國肯特時，她的父親James Buck27歲，母親Lydia Hartshorn20歲。她於大約1640年與Thomas Hartshorne在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1659年3月18日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁享年36歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Susanna Buck于1622年7月1日出生在英国肯特时，她的父亲James Buck27岁，母亲Lydia Hartshorn20岁。她于大约1640年与Thomas Hartshorne在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1659年3月18日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁享年36岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可雷亞丁。

nameMeanings.name: Buck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English buc(ke) ‘male goat’ (Old English bucca) or a ‘male deer’ (Old English bucc). The goat was popularly associated with lecherous behaviour and the deer with timidity and speed. The surname may also be a shortened form of longer occupational names, for example Roger le Bucmanger' ‘dealer in bucks or venison’, (Warwickshire 1221) or Walter Bucswayn perhaps ‘goatherd’ (Somerset 1327).
English: topographic name for someone who lived near a prominent beech tree, such as Peter atte Buk (Suffolk 1327), from Middle English buk ‘beech’ (from Old English bōc).
German and Dutch (Limburg): from a personal name, a short form of Burkhard (see Burkhart ).

variants.name: Buckson
meanings: English: either a patronymic from   Buck  , orpossibly an altered form of   Buxton  .

variants.name: Burkhart
meanings: German: from the medieval personal name Burkhard, from ancient Germanic Burghard, composed of the elements burg ‘fort, castle’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This form of the surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and in Czechia (compare 2 below), but it is very rare there. Compare also Borgardt , Brookhart , Burgert , Burghart , Burkart , and Burkett .
Germanized form of Polish and Czech Burkert , Czech Purkert or Purkart, and Slovenian Purkart: from a German variant and Slavicized forms of the medieval German personal name Burkhard (see 1 above).

variants.name: Buxton
meanings: habitational name from Buxton in Derbyshire, which in Middle English was called Buchestanes, Bucstones (i.e. ‘bowing stones’, from Middle English b(o)ugen, Old English būgan ‘to bow’ + stanes ‘stones’). It is probably named for logan stones in the vicinity. (Logan stones are boulders so poised that they rock at a touch.)
less commonly, a habitational name from Buxton in Norfolk, which is named with the genitive case of the Old English personal name Bucc (see Buck 1) + Old English tūn ‘settlement, enclosure’.

variants.name: Buckman
meanings: English: occupational name for a goatherd, Middle English bukkeman (from Old English bucca ‘he-goat’ + mann ‘man’).
English: occupational name for a scholar, scribe student, or bookbinder, from Middle English bok, buk(e) ‘book’ + man (from Old English bōc ‘book’ + mann ‘man’).
Americanized form of German Buckmann or Bückmann: derivative of Buck .

variants.name: Boughton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so named. Those in Cambridgeshire (formerly Huntingdonshire), Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, and Nottinghamshire are named from Old English bucc ‘goat’ or the Old English byname Bucca with the same meaning (see Buck 1) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’; those in Cheshire and Kent are named with Old English bōc ‘beech’ + tūn.

variants.name: Buckmaster
meanings: English (southeastern): habitational name from Buckminster in Leicestershire, named with the Old English personal name Bucca (see Buck ) + Old English mynster ‘minster, large church’.

variants.name: Bucknell
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Bucknell in Oxfordshire and Shropshire, Bucknall in Lincolnshire and Somerset, or Bucknowl in Dorset. These are all named with the Old English byname Bucca (see Buck ) or Old English bucca ‘he-goat’ (with genitive -n) + hyll ‘hill’ in the first two examples or healh ‘nook, hollow’ in the latter two. Bucknowl in Dorset, recorded as Bubecnolle in 1285, is named from the Old English personal name Bubba (see Bubb ) + cnoll ‘hill top’.

nameMeanings.name: Susanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Suzannah
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

variants.name: Suzanna
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Susanna Buck was born on 1 July 1622, in Kent, England, her father, James Buck, was 27 and her mother, Lydia Hartshorn, was 20. She married Thomas Hartshorne about 1640, in Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 18 March 1659, in Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 36, and was buried in Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Susanna Buck est née le 1 juillet 1622, à Kent, Angleterre, son père, James Buck, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Lydia Hartshorn, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Hartshorne environ 1640, à Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 18 mars 1659, à Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterrée à Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Susanna Buck am 1. Juli 1622 in Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James Buck 27 und ihre Mutter Lydia Hartshorn 20. Sie hat um 1640 in Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Thomas Hartshorne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 18. it.lifeSummary: Quando Susanna Buck è nata il il 1 luglio 1622 nel Kent, England, sua padre, James Buck, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Lydia Hartshorn, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Hartshorne circa 1640, nel Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 18 marzo 1659, nel Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolta a Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Susanna Buckが1622年7月1日にイギリスケントで生まれた時，父James Buckは27歳，母Lydia Hartshornは20歳であった。約1640年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスレアディングでThomas Hartshorneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1659年3月18日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスレアディングで36歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスレアディングにて埋葬された。

ko.Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Co: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 리딩
ko.lifeSummary: Susanna Buck(이)가 1622년 7월 1일 영국 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Buck(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lydia Hartshorn(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1640년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 리딩에서 Thomas Hartshorne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1659년 3월 18일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 리딩에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 리딩에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Susanna Buck nasceu em 1 de julho de 1622, em Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, James Buck, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Lydia Hartshorn, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Hartshorne aproximadamente 1640, em Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 18 de março de 1659, em Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 36 anos, e foi sepultada em Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Co: ридинг, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Susanna Buck родилась 1 июля 1622 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени James Buck было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Lydia Hartshorn, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Hartshorne около 1640 г. в местности ридинг, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 18 марта 1659 г. в местности ридинг, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 36 лет и была похоронена в местности ридинг, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Susanna Buck nació el 1 de julio de 1622, en Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, James Buck, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Lydia Hartshorn, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Thomas Hartshorne aproximadamente 1640, en Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de marzo de 1659, en Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultada en Reading, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
